cards.name: Auitzotl

abilities.name: Cocoxcamanilia
abilities.text: After you hit with a basic attack, when you make your next attack and defense rolls, roll one additional die and choose one die to exclude from the roll.

cards.flavor: Arguably the greatest military leadeer of pre-Columbian Mesoamerica, Auitzotl more than doubled the size of the Aztec empire during his reign.
cards.background: Auítzotl ascended to the eighth kingship of the Aztec Empire in 1486 AD. With Aztec power in question due to the weak rule of the previous ruler, Tízoc, Auítzotl immediately set about restoring his kingdom. After putting down a Huastec rebellion, he initiated a series of campaigns along Mexico’s Pacific coast, swiftly conquering the Zapotec and Mixtec tribes. Auítzotl also oversaw considerable renovations in Tenochtitlán. By the end of his reign, he had more than doubled the size of his empire, rebuilt his capital, and raised the Aztec Empire to the heights of its power. Use Auítzotl, the eighth Chief Speaker of the Aztec Empire and its greatest war-leader, to dominate your opponents in the Anachronism arena, and to claim it as your own.
cards.raw_source_text: Cocoxcamanilia: After you hit with a basic attack, when you make your next attack and defense rolls, roll one additional die and choose one die to exclude from the roll.

cards.name: Tlaloc

abilities.name: Tlacelmilhuiliztli – Reveal
abilities.text: At the end of this round, if you took more damage from opponents than you dealt, your attacks deal +1 damage for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: Rain god and ruler over the third of five worlds, Tlaloc was greatly feared as the bearer of drought and flood, despite his associations with fertility and life.
cards.raw_source_text: Tlacelmilhuiliztli – Reveal: At the end of this round, if you took more damage from opponents than you dealt, your attacks deal +1 damage for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Atlatl

abilities.name: Iciuhcac
abilities.text: You may use another weapon.

abilities.name: Tlacahuatiliztli
abilities.text: When you hit an opponent with this weapon, the defender’s support cards get –3 initiative for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: By compressing a wave of energy along the length of a wooden dart, the atlatl could hurl its missle over great distance and with exceptional power.
cards.raw_source_text:
Iciuhcac: You may use another weapon.
Tlacahuatiliztli: When you hit an opponent with this weapon, the defender’s support cards get –3 initiative for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Chimalli

abilities.name: Machtitenahuatiliztli
abilities.text: When you are attacked, if you are in the attacker’s basic attack grid, add the modifier from your space in the attacker’s basic attack grid to your first defense roll.

cards.flavor: Aztec shields grew in beauty and extavagance with military rank. Of the eight surviving pieces of Aztec featherwork, five are chimalli.
cards.raw_source_text: Machtitenahuatiliztli: When you are attacked, if you are in the attacker’s basic attack grid, add the modifier from your space in the attacker’s basic attack grid to your first defense roll.

cards.name: Mectal

abilities.name: Tlatzoyotiliztli
abilities.text: If an opponent has more than one card that requires hands to use, that player must discard one of those cards.

abilities.name: Tetzicoliztli
abilities.text: When any opponent attacks with a two-handed weapon, their attack rolls get –2.

cards.flavor: The number of captured enemies a warrior had accrued determined his military rank. Mectal were used to bind captives who had been immobilized during battle.
cards.raw_source_text:
Tlatzoyotiliztli: If an opponent has more than one card that requires hands to use, that player must discard one of those cards.
Tetzicoliztli: When any opponent attacks with a two-handed weapon, their attack rolls get –2.

cards.name: Cuitlahuac

abilities.name: Amana
abilities.text: Once each round, after you hit with a basic attack, you may immediately make an attack with one of your weapons, if possible. This attack cannot deal more than its base damage.

cards.flavor: Succeeding his brother Moctezuma II, Cuitlahuac led a major Aztec victory against the Spanish, in which 400 conquistadors and thousands of their Mesoamerican allies were slain.
cards.background: Upon the mortal wounding of his brother, Moctezuma II, Cuitláhuac took power. A cunning and ferocious warrior, he immediately set about visiting terrible retribution upon the Spanish invaders. On the night of July 1st, 1520, he oversaw a major Aztec victory, in which over 400 Spanish conquistadors and literally thousands of their Mesoamerican allies were slain by a vengeful Aztec army. So utter and terrible was the defeat that the Spanish called it La Noche Triste, the Night of Sorrow. Despite this victory, Cuitláhuac could not overcome the inevitable and, in October that year, Tenochtitlán came under siege. Disease proved to be the Aztec’s undoing, with smallpox killing thousands of Aztecs, including Cuitláhuac himself. Use Cuitláhuac, restored to vigor, to heap vengeance and sorrow without end upon your opponents in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Amana: Once each round, after you hit with a basic attack, you may immediately make an attack with one of your weapons, if possible. This attack cannot deal more than its base damage.

cards.name: Tezcatlipoca

abilities.name: Teochihua
abilities.text: Your first attack roll each round gains +1.

abilities.name: Telchihua
abilities.text: After an opponent makes a roll, if the roll is less than the opponent's initiative for the round, you may discard this card. If you do so, that player must discard one of their non-weapon support cards.

cards.flavor: The god of beauty, night and war, his smoking mirror killed all who inhaled its fumes. The twin of Quetalcoatl, together they captured the great monster Cipatli and created the lands from her body.
cards.raw_source_text:
Teochihua: Your first attack roll each round gains +1.
Telchihua: After an opponent makes a roll, if the roll is less than the opponent's initiative for the round, you may discard this card. If you do so, that player must discard one of their non-weapon support cards.

cards.name: Tlacochtli

abilities.name: Mina
abilities.text: Before you make an attack with this weapon, you may discard this card. If you do, your attack roll gains +3 or the roll counts as doubles.

cards.flavor: Tipped with stone, copper or obsidian, these short spears were preferred in close combat, where longer weapons proved less effective.
cards.raw_source_text: Mina: Before you make an attack with this weapon, you may discard this card. If you do, your attack roll gains +3 or the roll counts as doubles.

cards.name: Ocelocolohtic

abilities.name: Tlamatiliztili
abilities.text: You gain +2 experience.

abilities.name: Oceloaacqui
abilities.text: After your are dealt damage, if you have been dealt more than 3 damage from any one opponent in that round, your next succesful attack is a critical hit.

cards.flavor: Worn by the Ocelomeh, or Jaguar Warriors, this armor marked its bearer out as one of the most fearsome of Aztec combatants.
cards.raw_source_text:
Tlamatiliztili - Reveal: You gain +2 experience.
Oceloaacqui: After your are dealt damage, if you have been dealt more than 3 damage from any one opponent in that round, your next succesful attack is a critical hit.

cards.name: Ocelomeh

abilities.name: Necalilizicniuhtli
abilities.text: Once each turn, your next basic attack deals +2 damage.

abilities.name: Altepecaconeteyaotlani
abilities.text: If you are an Aztec warrior, your first attack roll each round gains +1.

cards.flavor: One of the two elite schools of Aztec warriors, only a soldier who had captured four enemies could become a Jaguar Warrior.
cards.raw_source_text:
Necalilizicniuhtli - Action: Once each turn, your next basic attack deals +2 damage.
Altepecaconeteyaotlani: If you are an Aztec warrior, your first attack roll each round gains +1.

cards.name: Itzcoatl

abilities.name: Ixhuacayotlani
abilities.text: After you hit with a basic attack, gain 2 experience.

cards.flavor: The first Aztec Emperor, Itzcoatl re-wrote the Aztec social, religious and governmental hierarchies, making military prowess the greatest route to power.
cards.background: Assuming the Aztec kingship after the capture and suicide of his nephew Chimalpopoca, Itzcóatl instituted enormous social changes that became the driving force behind a century of expansion and conquest. He created a feudal system that concentrated power in the hands of the ruling classes and, by creating a system of reward based on taking captives in battle, he made military prowess a key path to gaining social rank. He even re-wrote Aztec history and religion to reinforce his radical reforms. By forging an alliance with two city-states, Itzcóatl conquered the Tepanecs in 1428, and ultimately elevated himself to the position of the first of the Aztec Emperors. Use Itzcóatl, the Obsidian Serpent, to rewrite history with the defeat of all who stand before you in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Ixhuacayotlani: After you hit with a basic attack, gain 2 experience.

cards.name: Teyaotenyiliztli

abilities.name: Patiyotl
abilities.text: Twice each round, if you are an Earth or Aether warrior, when you hit an adjacent opponent who is not in your basic attack grid with a basic attack, deal 1 damage to the defender.

cards.flavor: Due to Itzcoatl's reforms, a warrior's prestige was tied directly to his achievemenets. Only by taking captives or defeating a great foe could a warrior improve his lot.
cards.raw_source_text: Patiyotl: Twice each round, if you are an Earth or Aether warrior, when you hit an adjacent opponent who is not in your basic attack grid with a basic attack, deal 1 damage to the defender.

cards.name: Macuahuitl

abilities.name: Atlacapetlachi
abilities.text: After you miss with this weapon, you may immediately make a basic attack against the same opponent at +1.

cards.flavor: Made of wood, and edged with wickedly sharp obsidian, the macuahuitl was primarily intended to maim opponents, who could later be captured.
cards.raw_source_text: Atlacapetlachi: After you miss with this weapon, you may immediately make a basic attack against the same opponent at +1.

cards.name: Cuauhcuachimalli

abilities.name: Tlamatiliztli – Reveal
abilities.text: You gain +2 Experience.

abilities.name: Cuauhchicauhtiliztli
abilities.text: If you are dealt more than 2 damage from any one opponent in a round, you may attack twice with one of your weapons on your next turn.

cards.flavor: Worn by the Cuauhcuauhtin, or Eagle Warriors, these helmets denoted a warrior of great prowess - and high social and military rank - who had captured many enemies.
cards.raw_source_text:
Tlamatiliztli – Reveal: You gain +2 Experience.
Cuauhchicauhtiliztli: If you are dealt more than 2 damage from any one opponent in a round, you may attack twice with one of your weapons on your next turn.

cards.name: Teponaztli

abilities.name: Amoixnahuamiquitlalhuacuicatl
abilities.text: If you are not a Fire or Metal warrior, damage from attacks cannot be reduced.

abilities.name: Teoyohtica
abilities.text: You may use another Special card in this game.

cards.flavor: Made from the hollowed section of a hardwood tree, the Aztec war drum bore carvings of mystical symbols and was respected as a spiritual being exiled to earth.
cards.raw_source_text:
Amoixnahuamiquitlalhuacuicatl: If you are not a Fire or Metal warrior, damage from attacks cannot be reduced.
Teoyohtica: You may use another Special card in this game.

cards.name: Moctezuma II

abilities.name: Tezcatiliztli
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you did not win initiative, gain +1 speed for that round.

cards.flavor: Depite extending the power of Tenochtitlan to dominate its sister cities of Texcoco and Tlacopan, Moctezuma's supernatural fear of the Spanish led to the downfall of his empire.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Tezcatiliztli: At the start of each round, if you did not win initiative, gain +1 speed for that round.

cards.name: Quetzalcoatl

abilities.name: Ixnamicealiztli
abilities.text: Ties are won by the warrior with less life instead of by the warrior with higher experience.

cards.flavor: According to myth, Quetzacoatl went to Mictlan, the underworld, and created mankind from his own blood and the bones of all previous races.
cards.raw_source_text: Ixnamicealiztli: Ties are won by the warrior with less life instead of by the warrior with higher experience.

cards.name: Tecpatl

abilities.name: Mictilia
abilities.text: When you hit with this weapon, if the defender has more life than your warrior, this attack deals +2 damage.

cards.flavor: Painstakingly chipped from obsidian glass and often decorated with religious syumbols, the edge of the tecpatl knife could cut like a razorblade.
cards.raw_source_text: Mictilia: When you hit with this weapon, if the defender has more life than your warrior, this attack deals +2 damage.

cards.name: Copilli

abilities.name: Ixcuepa
abilities.text: Twice each turn, if you have previously been hit this turn, you may re-roll your defense roll.

cards.flavor: The feather crown, made of gold and over 400 quetzal feathers, was a symbol of kingship that bore strong spiritual significance.
cards.raw_source_text: Ixcuepa: Twice each turn, if you have previously been hit this turn, you may re-roll your defense roll.

cards.name: Tlimatli

abilities.name: Cempacca
abilities.text: Once each turn, after one or more of your dice are re-rolled, the roll gains +2.

cards.flavor: The tlimatli was a blue and white mantle that denoted the high-ranking status of its wearer. It was folded over the shoulders and fastened by a green clasp called a chalchivitl.
cards.raw_source_text: Cempacca: Once each turn, after one or more of your dice are re-rolled, the roll gains +2.

cards.name: Tlacaelel

abilities.name: Ohuipetlachiuh
abilities.text: When you hit with a basic attack, the defender chooses to either get –1 to all of their rolls on their next turn or for you to gain 1 life. If the attack was a critical hit, apply both options.

cards.flavor: Inexorable and cruel, Tlacaelel elevated the bloodthirsty cult of Huitzilopochtli by dedicating the seventh reconstruction of the Templo Mayor to him.
cards.background: Further elevating the bloodthirsty cult of Huitzilopochtli, which his uncle Itzcóatl had identified with the sun god, Tlacaelel instituted the Xochivaovotl, or Flower Wars, as a means of both demonstrating Aztec military power and taking captives. He also began a systematic marrying of Aztec nobles to the aristocratic Toltec families of Cholula, as a means of elevating the lineage of his people. Having ordered the destruction of all records that made reference to the humble origins of the Aztecs, he also incorporated elements of Toltec ideology into Aztec culture. Inexorable in his desire to see his people exalted, he erased histories and spilled the blood of thousands to ensure that the sun would not falter in its path across the sky. Use Tlacaelel, High Priest and Grand Vizier of the Aztecs, to drive ineluctably towards a greatness built on noble heritage and the blood of your fallen enemies.
cards.raw_source_text: Ohuipetlachiuh: When you hit with a basic attack, the defender chooses to either get –1 to all of their rolls on their next turn or for you to gain 1 life. If the attack was a critical hit, apply both options.

cards.name: Huitzilopochtli

abilities.name: Axittomoctli
abilities.text: At the end of each round, deal 1 damage to all adjacent non-Fire warriors.

cards.flavor: God of war, sun and death, he allowed the bravest men who had fallen in battle, and the bravest women, who had died in childbirth, to return to earth as butterflies.
cards.raw_source_text: Axittomoctli: At the end of each round, deal 1 damage to all adjacent non-Fire warriors.

cards.name: Tematlatl

abilities.name: Quilitli
abilities.text: You may use another weapon.

abilities.name: Zotlahuaxoxoliztli
abilities.text: If you hit with this weapon, the defenders next defense roll gets –2.

cards.flavor: Aztec warriors used to heat the stones hurled by these slings to a scalding temperature so that the missiles would burn through enemies upon impact.
cards.raw_source_text:
Quilitli: You may use another weapon.
Zotlahuaxoxoliztli: If you hit with this weapon, the defenders next defense roll gets –2.

cards.name: Ichcahuipilli

abilities.name: Cuachicpalli
abilities.text: Once each round, after you hit a warrior with a basic attack, your defense rolls against that warrior gain +2 until the start of your next turn

cards.flavor: Ichcahuipilli armor was constructed from linen and was usually soaked in brine, which toughened it significantly against the blows of an enemy.
cards.raw_source_text: Cuachicpalli: Once each round, after you hit a warrior with a basic attack, your defense rolls against that warrior gain +2 until the start of your next turn

cards.name: Xochivaovotl

abilities.name: Aacquicualnezcayotl – Action
abilities.text: Once each round, make a basic attack against an adjacent opponent at +1. If the attack hits, gain 1 life. Otherwise, your attack rolls this round gain +1.

cards.flavor: Instituted as a kind of ritual warfare, the "Flower Wars" were waged to gain captives, whose blood would fuel the sun and postpone the end of the world.
cards.raw_source_text: Aacquicualnezcayotl – Action: Once each round, make a basic attack against an adjacent opponent at +1. If the attack hits, gain 1 life. Otherwise, your attack rolls this round gain +1.

cards.name: Hijikata Toshizo

abilities.name: Taikyuutroku
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you won initiative, attacks against you cannot deal critical hits or deal more than their base damage.

cards.flavor:
Though my body may decay on the island of Ezo,
My spirit guards my lord in the east.- death poem of Hijikata Toshizo
cards.background: Born to a farming family, Toshizo grew up a spoiled and mean child. However, upon witnessing the seppuku of an Aizu samurai, who opposed the Meiji Restoration, Toshizo was overcome with grief. Later that year, he founded the Shinsengumi with a man called Kondo Isami, which acted as a police force that fought Imperial Loyalist activities in Kyoto. Hijikata performed his duties with utter conviction, even ordering friends to commit seppuku where the rules of Shinsengumi demanded it. When it became apparent that the Meiji Restoration was on the verge of triumph, he marched into battle one last time, saying that he went to fight harder and better than he ever had before, and to die for his country. Use Hijikata Toshizo, the Demon of the Shinsengumi, to overcome weakness, to fight with conviction, and to give all in the name of defiance and honor.
cards.raw_source_text: Taikyuutroku: At the start of each round, if you won initiative, attacks against you cannot deal critical hits or deal more than their base damage.

cards.name: Kyokuchu-Hatto

abilities.name: Chuuseo
abilities.text: Warriors cannot gain speed. Each time an opposing warrior reveals a support card of a type that they already have in play, they get -1 speed that round.

cards.flavor: Hijikata Toshizo helped found the Shinsengumi organization. Anyone who performed any action contrary to the way of the samurai, or attempted to leave the group, was forced to commit seppuku.
cards.raw_source_text: Chuuseo: Warriors cannot gain speed. Each time an opposing warrior reveals a support card of a type that they already have in play, they get -1 speed that round.

cards.name: Izuminokami-Kanesada

abilities.name: Ryuudouteki na Kougeki
abilities.text: After you miss with this weapon, you may move one space.

abilities.name: Hayauchi
abilities.text: The next card you reveal gains +5 initiative.

cards.flavor: Among the many kantana that Toshizo wielded, this one he used the longest, finally giving it to a servant three months before his death.
cards.raw_source_text:
Ryuudouteki na Kougeki: After you miss with this weapon, you may move one space.
Hayauchi: The next card you reveal gains +5 initiative.

cards.name: Haori

abilities.name: Gishiki
abilities.text: Gain +3 experience.

abilities.name: Dentou
abilities.text: Your other support cards gain +2 initiative. Opponents cannot reduce damage from your basic attacks.

cards.flavor: All of the men of Shinsengumi wore this robe as a uniform. So terrifying was this group that even the sight of this unifrom was enough to cause fear.
cards.raw_source_text:
Gishiki - Reveal: Gain +3 experience.
Dentou: Your other support cards gain +2 initiative. Opponents cannot reduce damage from your basic attacks.

cards.name: Ishida-Sanyaku

abilities.name: Doutei
abilities.text: If your first action on any of your turns is to move, your attack rolls that turn gain +2.

cards.flavor: Toshizo sold medicine called "ishida-sanyaku" in his early days, and later used this trade as a disguise to cover his espionage activities.
cards.raw_source_text: Doutei: If your first action on any of your turns is to move, your attack rolls that turn gain +2.

cards.name: Minamoto no Yoshitsune

abilities.name: Mukouryokuka
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you won initiative, opponents cannot attack you with weapons that round.

cards.flavor: Yoshitsune battled alongside with elder brother Yorimoto in the name of the Genji family. When Yorimoto later went on to usurp Imperial power, Yoshitsune fought against him, only to come to a tagic end.
cards.background: Minamoto no Yoshitsune was a great general and warrior during the late Heian and early Kamakura Periods in Japan. He fought alongside his elder brother, Yoritomo, against the Taira Clan, but later turned to fight against him, after Yoritomo moved to usurp Imperial power. Failing to defeat his brother, who installed himself as the first Shogun in Japan’s history, Yoshitsune spent the rest of his life fleeing from Yoritomo’s men. On his travels, he attracted many retainers, including the warrior monk Saito Musashibo Benkei, who swore lifelong fealty. It is said that their loyalty was so great that, when escape finally became impossible, they held off a force many times greater than their own number to allow Yoshitsune to commit seppuku. Use Minamoto no Yoshitsune, humble and dedicated, to oppose those so eager to win power and fame in the Anachronism arena that they would sacrifice all nobility and worth to do so.
cards.raw_source_text: Mukouryokuka: At the start of each round, if you won initiative, opponents cannot attack you with weapons that round.

cards.name: Genji

abilities.name: Hangeki
abilities.text: If you are hit twice in one round, during the next round, you gain +1 speed and your attack rolls gain +1.

cards.flavor: The royal family granted the Genji name, which became one of the most important factions of the Heian era.
cards.raw_source_text: Hangeki: If you are hit twice in one round, during the next round, you gain +1 speed and your attack rolls gain +1.

cards.name: Usumidori

abilities.name: Subayai Kougeki
abilities.text: When you hit with this weapon, the attack and defender both roll one die and add his or her warrior's experience. If your total is higher, gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.flavor: Yorimoto gave Usumidori to his brother, Yoshitsune, at a time when the katana had just started to become popular among the samurai.
cards.raw_source_text: Subayai Kougeki: When you hit with this weapon, the attack and defender both roll one die and add his or her warrior's experience. If your total is higher, gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.name: Akaitoodoshi-Yoroi

abilities.name: Saikaku na Soubi
abilities.text: If you have a sword in play, your attack and defense rolls gain +1.

cards.flavor: It was said that Yoshitsune wore this yoroi at the battle of Dan-no-Ura, where he performed the famous "hassotobi," a great leap over 8 boats.
cards.raw_source_text: Saikaku na Soubi: If you have a sword in play, your attack and defense rolls gain +1.

cards.name: Rikutosanryaku

abilities.name: Mittsu no Himtsu
abilities.text: After an attack of defense roll is made, you may discard this card. If you do, that player's next roll of the same type is set to the value of the current roll -1.

cards.flavor: Derived from the Liutao Sanlue, a famous Zhou Dynasty book of Chinese political and military strategy, every word of the Rikutosanryaku was embedded in Yoshitsune's mind.
cards.raw_source_text: Mittsu no Himtsu: After an attack of defense roll is made, you may discard this card. If you do, that player's next roll of the same type is set to the value of the current roll -1.

cards.name: Sanada Nobushige

abilities.name: Kandai na Bougyo
abilities.text: When you hit an opponent, you may choose for the attack to deal -1 damage, to a minimum of 0. If you do, reduce the damage of the next attack that hits you by 2, to a minimum of 0.

cards.flavor: Sanada Nobushige was the last and greatest threat to Tokugawa Ieyasu. Despite being his enemy, Ieyasu respected Nobushige’s abilities enormously, and praised his great worth as an adversary.
cards.background: N/A Promo Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Kandai na Bougyo: When you hit an opponent, you may choose for the attack to deal -1 damage, to a minimum of 0. If you do, reduce the damage of the next attack that hits you by 2, to a minimum of 0.

cards.name: Rokunmonsenmon

abilities.name: Rokumonsen
abilities.text: You cannot discard this card.

abilities.name: Kesshi no Kakugo
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 and your attacks deal +1 damage. After you make an attack, add a counter to this card. When you place the sixth counter on this card, you lose the game.

cards.flavor: The Sanada family crest is derived from a Buddhist story, describing why one should take six coins to go through hell. It warned all enemies that no Sanada feared death.
cards.raw_source_text:
Rokumonsen: You cannot discard this card.
Kesshi no Kakugo: Your attack rolls gain +1 and your attacks deal +1 damage. After you make an attack, add a counter to this card. When you place the sixth counter on this card, you lose the game.

cards.name: Muramasa

abilities.name: Densetsuteki
abilities.text: This weapon's damage cannot be increased or reduced by card abilities.

cards.flavor: Tradition holds that this katana was cursed. Certainly, it brought nothing but woe to the Tokugawa family.
cards.raw_source_text: Densetsuteki: This weapon's damage cannot be increased or reduced by card abilities.

cards.name: Akazonae

abilities.name: Azayaka na Aka
abilities.text: At the start of each round, roll one die. If the number is odd, your defense rolls gain +1 this round. Otherwise, your attack rolls gain +1 this round. If you have a Japanese inspiration and the roll is greater than 3, you gain +1 speed this round.

cards.flavor: The soldiers of Sanada Nobushige's army armored themselves entirely in red during their war against Tokugawa Ieyasu.
cards.raw_source_text: Azayaka na Aka: At the start of each round, roll one die. If the number is odd, your defense rolls gain +1 this round. Otherwise, your attack rolls gain +1 this round. If you have a Japanese inspiration and the roll is greater than 3, you gain +1 speed this round.

cards.name: Sanadamaru

abilities.name: Toride
abilities.text: Once each game, after you are hit, if this card is in play or in your discard pile, you may have the hit deal -1 damage, to a minimum of 0. If this card is in your discard pile, you may not use this ability again this match.

cards.flavor: Sanadamaru was an invincible fort, built by Nobushige to the south of Osaka Castle. The Tokugawa army met defeat in every attempt to capture it.
cards.raw_source_text: Toride: Once each game, after you are hit, if this card is in play or in your discard pile, you may have the hit deal -1 damage, to a minimum of 0. If this card is in your discard pile, you may not use this ability again this match.

cards.name: Takeda Shingen

abilities.name: Saisho no Ichigeki
abilities.text: If you make the first attack of the game, and that attack hits, you gain 2 life and your attack rolls gain +1 for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: Often referred to as “The Tiger of Kai,” Takeda Shingen was a great warlord and politician during the Sengoku Era, and the great rival of Uesugi Kenshin.
cards.background: After taking control of his clan, Takeda Shingen set about conquering Shinano Province. He ambushed the armies sent against him and soon conquered the region. He then faced Uesugi Kenshin, who was to become his greatest rival, and whom he fought against in five separate wars. Neither could gain the upper hand, and each developed great respect for the other. When Oda Nobunaga and Tokugawa Ieyasu threatened to conquer all of Japan, Shingen stepped forward as the only warlord with the power and ability to halt them. Bringing them to battle at Mikata-ga-hara, he routed their combined forces with ease, but died unexpectedly in 1573 as he launched his next campaign. Use Takeda Shingen, the Tiger of Kai, to strike your enemy when he least expects, and to earn the respect due to the greatest of warriors in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Saisho no Ichigeki: If you make the first attack of the game, and that attack hits, you gain 2 life and your attack rolls gain +1 for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Furinkazan

abilities.name: Roukou
abilities.text: Your warrior gains the following ability: "Roukou: You win ties against warriors without the Roukou ability."

cards.flavor: Inspired by the "Art of War," Furkinizan stands for "Wind, Grove, Fire, and Mountain," and described Takeda's approach to the ways of war and politics.
cards.raw_source_text: Roukou: Your warrior gains the following ability: "Roukou: You win ties against warriors without the Roukou ability."

cards.name: Jin-Dachi

abilities.name: Sinchou na Kougeki
abilities.text: When you hit with this weapon, the attacker and defender each roll one die and add his or her experience. If your total is higher, your attack rolls gain +1 for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: The jin-dachi was a type of kantana that saw common use during the Sengoku Era. Over two meters in length, it was used both on foot and while mounted.
cards.raw_source_text: Sinchou na Kougeki: When you hit with this weapon, the attacker and defender each roll one die and add his or her experience. If your total is higher, your attack rolls gain +1 for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Touseigusoku

abilities.name: Danryokusei
abilities.text: After you are hit, your defense rolls gain +1 to a maximum of a +3 bonus from this card.

cards.flavor: The Sengoku Era saw great strides in the production of effective armor. Touseigusoku armor was lighter than previous armors and covered the entire body.
cards.raw_source_text: Danryokusei: After you are hit, your defense rolls gain +1 to a maximum of a +3 bonus from this card.

cards.name: Gunpai

abilities.name: Senjutsu
abilities.text: If you have an Armor card in play, your rolls that are doubles gain +2, and your opponent’s rolls that are doubles get –2.

cards.flavor: Samurai war leaders used the gunpai to direct their forces on the battlefield.
cards.raw_source_text: Senjutsu: If you have an Armor card in play, your rolls that are doubles gain +2, and your opponent’s rolls that are doubles get –2.

cards.name: Uesugi Kenshin

abilities.name: Ikusa-Gami
abilities.text: If the first attack against you this game hits, you gain +4 experience and +1 speed for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: A warlord of the Sengoku Era. Uesugi was called the "God of War" and "The Dragon of Echigo," due to his master of strategy, combat, and his great personal charisma.
cards.background: Called “The Dragon of Echigo,” due to his fearsome martial prowess, Uesugi Kenshin was renowned for his devotion to Bishamonten, the god of war. While in his mid-teens, he took control of his clan from his brother and began to cement his power. The victories of Takeda Shingen in Shinano Province presented a great threat, and so Kenshin moved against the great warlord. This led to a series of five wars, in which Kenshin came closer than anyone else to defeating the Tiger of Kai. After Shingen died, Kenshin fought against Oda Nobunaga, defeating him at the battle of Tedorigawa, but poor health caught up with him as he prepared to mount a large-scale offensive. Use Uesugi Kenshin, the Dragon of Echigo, to bring your enemies to the point of your blade and defeat all who would oppose your skill in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Ikusa-Gami: If the first attack against you this game hits, you gain +4 experience and +1 speed for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Kawanakajima

abilities.name: Ittei no Shouri
abilities.text: If you did not win initiative for the current round, your attack rolls gain +2.

abilities.name: Tatakai ni Fukki
abilities.text: Once each match, after one of your other cards is discarded, you may put this card into play from your discard pile.

cards.flavor: The Kawanakajima–no–Tatakai was a series of five wars fought between Uesugi Kenshin and Takeda Shingen, in which both warlords fought each other to a stalemate.
cards.raw_source_text:
Ittei no Shouri: If you did not win initiative for the current round, your attack rolls gain +2. 
Tatakai ni Fukki: Once each match, after one of your other cards is discarded, you may put this card into play from your discard pile.

cards.name: Azukinagamitsu

abilities.name: Kyouiteki na Surudosa
abilities.text: When you attack with this weapon, if the attack roll is greater than the higher of the defender's experience or the defense roll without bonuses, deal 1 damage to the defender.

cards.flavor: Azukinagamitsu was so sharp that it was said to be able to cut an akuzi bean in half just by touching it.
cards.raw_source_text: Kyouiteki na Surudosa: When you attack with this weapon, if the attack roll is greater than the higher of the defender's experience or the defense roll without bonuses, deal 1 damage to the defender.

cards.name: Kusubekawasugakeodoshi

abilities.name: Juusoubi
abilities.text: Reduce the damage you take from opposing card abilities by 1, to a minimum of 0.

abilities.name: Fukushuu
abilities.text: After you are damaged by an opponent’s non–weapon support card, you may discard this card to discard the opposing card.

cards.flavor: Uesugi Kenshin’s armor was made from the most rare and precious materials, some of which were provided by Oda Nobunaga himself.
cards.raw_source_text:
Juusoubi: Reduce the damage you take from opposing card abilities by 1, to a minimum of 0. 
Fukushuu: After you are damaged by an opponent’s non–weapon support card, you may discard this card to discard the opposing card.

cards.name: Hata Bi

abilities.name: Senshi no Inori – Reveal
abilities.text: If you took the last turn of the previous round and won initiative this round, your attack rolls this round that are less than 9 are set to 9.

abilities.name: Netsujou
abilities.text: You may use another Special card in this game.

cards.flavor: People thought of Uesugi Kenshin as a reincarnation of Bishamonten, from which the word "Bi" was derived.
cards.raw_source_text:
Senshi no Inori – Reveal: If you took the last turn of the previous round and won initiative this round, your attack rolls this round that are less than 9 are set to 9. 
Netsujou: You may use another Special card in this game.

cards.name: Amr ibn al'Aas

abilities.name: Musaada
abilities.text: At the start of each round, you may choose another warrior. That warrior’s defense rolls gain +1 that round.

abilities.name: Motatarref
abilities.text: Once each round, if you have an Inspiration in play, you may re–roll one of your defense rolls.

cards.flavor: Amr ibn al–Aas was converted to the Muslim faith by An–Najaashiy in Abyssinia, and subsequently became one of the greatest warriors and generals of his day.
cards.background: A contemporary of the prophet Muhammad, Amr ibn Al-Aas initially fought against Islam, but was converted to the Muslim faith by An-Najaashiy in Abyssinia. Leading the Arab invasion of Egypt, Amr achieved a relatively bloodless conquest of the lands to the east of Cairo. With a stronghold established at Misr al-Fustat, “The Tented City”, the first mosque on the African continent was built, reputedly on the site where Amr had pitched his tent. He then led the conquest of Alexandria in 642, aided by the Coptic Christian population, who suffered frequent persecution at the hands of Alexandria’s Byzantine rulers. Although unquestionably one of the most successful Islamic commanders of his time, divisions remain today as to his worthiness. Use Amr ibn al-Aas, commander of the faithful, to achieve victories on the sands of the Anachronism arena that will last through time.
cards.raw_source_text:
Musaada: At the start of each round, you may choose another warrior. That warrior’s defense rolls gain +1 that round. 
Motatarref: Once each round, if you have an Inspiration in play, you may re–roll one of your defense rolls.

cards.name: Masjid ahl ar-Rayah

abilities.name: Taabbud – Reveal
abilities.text: Gain 1 life.

abilities.name: Adfaa Ehtramat
abilities.text: Once each match, at the start of a round, if this card is in your discard pile and you have less life than any opponent, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: Erected on the spot where Amr ibn al'Aas pitched his tent at Fustat, this mosque was the first to be built, not just in Egypt, but on the enitre African continent.
cards.raw_source_text:
Taabbud – Reveal: Gain 1 life. 
Adfaa Ehtramat: Once each match, at the start of a round, if this card is in your discard pile and you have less life than any opponent, gain 1 life.

cards.name: Ras al Asad

abilities.name: Mukhief
abilities.text: Attack rolls with this weapon that are less than the defender's initiative are set to the defender's initiative.

cards.flavor: The lion was a symbol of courage and power, and adorned the heads of many Muslim maces.
cards.raw_source_text: Mukhief: Attack rolls with this weapon that are less than the defender's initiative are set to the defender's initiative.

cards.name: Al Derba

abilities.name: Darb
abilities.text: When you are hit, if the defense roll plus three is greater than the attack roll, deal one damage to the attacker.

cards.flavor: As well as being more resilient, a shield with a boss could be used to bash an opponent more effectively than one without.
cards.raw_source_text: Darb: When you are hit, if the defense roll plus three is greater than the attack roll, deal one damage to the attacker.

cards.name: Reaaia Elahiia

abilities.name: Saleh
abilities.text: After you make a defense roll, you may discard this card or one of your Inspiration cards. If you do, set one die of the defense roll to any number.

cards.flavor: "Allah, I am not innocent, so forgive me. I am not mighty, so help me. And if Your mercy does not come to me, I will surely be of those destroyed." - Amr ibn al-A'as
cards.raw_source_text: Saleh: After you make a defense roll, you may discard this card or one of your Inspiration cards. If you do, set one die of the defense roll to any number.

cards.name: Harun al-Rashid

abilities.name: Ghazu
abilities.text: At the end of each round, choose an opponent. That opponent may choose to get -4 initiative on the next round. If they do not, you gain +1 speed for the next round.

cards.flavor: "Harun al-Rashid, Commander of the Faithful to Nicephorus, the Roman dog: I have read thy letter. Thou shalt not hear, thou shalt see my reply." - Harun al-Rashid
cards.background: The fifth Abbasid Caliph, Harun al-Rashid was both a devoted scholar and an unforgiving military leader. He invited learned individuals to his courts, opening up diplomatic relations across Europe and Asia, and quickly established his military credentials by securing surrender and tribute from Empress Irene of the Eastern Roman Empire. When Nicephorus usurped her position and rebuked Harun, his response was swift, sieging and sacking Heraclea. Nicephorus agreed to pay tribute, but immediately reneged, causing Harun to invade again. He defeated an army of 125,000 with only 15,000, and wounded Nicephorus himself. Today, Harun is best remembered as the caliph upon whom the One Thousand and One Nights is based. Use Harun al-Rashid, caliph of a thousand wonders, to outthink and overcome your opponent in the Anchronism arena, no matter the odds stacked against you.
cards.raw_source_text: Ghazu: At the end of each round, choose an opponent. That opponent may choose to get -4 initiative on the next round. If they do not, you gain +1 speed for the next round.

cards.name: Al-Khayzuran

abilities.name: Reaaia
abilities.text: Once this game, before initiative is determined for a round, if you revealed a support card that round, set your opponent’s initiative for that round to your life.

abilities.name: Thanaa
abilities.text: If you are a Wind warrior, your first roll of each turn gains +1.

cards.flavor: A slave girl born in Yemen, Al–Khayzuran rose to power through strength of personality, and exerted great influence during the reigns of her husband and sons.
cards.raw_source_text:
Reaaia: Once this game, before initiative is determined for a round, if you revealed a support card that round, set your opponent’s initiative for that round to your life. 
Thanaa: If you are a Wind warrior, your first roll of each turn gains +1.

cards.name: Kaws

abilities.name: Galied – Action
abilities.text: Once each round, if you have a cavalry card in play, make an attack with this weapon at +2.

abilities.name: Pasche
abilities.text: Critical hits with this weapon deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: The use of the bow by Muslim cavalry was rooted in the antecedent tactics of raiding desert nomads.
cards.raw_source_text:
Galied – Action: Once each round, if you have a cavalry card in play, make an attack with this weapon at +2.

cards.name: Ran

abilities.name: Talik
abilities.text: You may use an another Armor card in this game. While you have two Armor cards, your attack rolls gain +1.

abilities.name: Anied
abilities.text: If you have less speed than the attacker, attacks against you deal –1 damage, to a minimum of 1.

cards.flavor: Ran were cavalry leggings, worn over or above boots and tied to a belt fastened around the waist. Although usually made of leather, iron ran were not unheard of.
cards.raw_source_text:
Talik: You may use an another Armor card in this game. While you have two Armor cards, your attack rolls gain +1. 
Anied: If you have less speed than the attacker, attacks against you deal –1 damage, to a minimum of 1.

cards.name: Alf Layla wa-Layla

abilities.name: Hekaiat Kabl Elnaum
abilities.text: At the end of each round, name a card type. After initiative is determined next round, any opponent who revealed a card of the named type chooses to either discard the card or get -2 to all rolls that round.

cards.flavor: "The Thousand and One Nights" is the collection of Arabian tales. With stories ranging from King Solomon to the kings on ancient Persia, from Alexander the Great to the grea caliphs, wonders drip from its every page.
cards.raw_source_text: Hekaiat Kabl Elnaum: At the end of each round, name a card type. After initiative is determined next round, any opponent who revealed a card of the named type chooses to either discard the card or get -2 to all rolls that round.

cards.name: Maowvia

abilities.name: Mutauag
abilities.text: When you are attacked, you may reduce the value of a die in the attack by 1.

cards.flavor: Reigning as Queen in the Bedouin Saracens, Maowvia led her armies to victory after victory over Roman armies, forcing the Emperor Valens to seek peace.
cards.background: Syrian Queen of the Bedouin Saracens, Maowvia rebelled repeatedly against Rome. Over the course of seven years, she led her armies to victories in Palestine, Phoenicia and Egypt. Despite Roman reinforcements, Maowvia continued to outmaneuver and defeat the Roman legions that marched against her. Eventually, Valens, Emperor of the Eastern Roman Empire, sought peace with her. Later, when the Goths began their attacks on the Roman frontier, she sent an army of cavalry to assist those who had been her enemy. By marrying her daughter to the Supreme Commander of the Eastern Roman army, and with the aid of St. Moses, “the Apostle of the Saracens”, Maowvia established stability for her people, which lasted long after her death. Use Maowvia, queen of the desert, to punish all those who would dare underestimate your ability in the Anachronism arena, and to achieve victory with the speed of the wind.
cards.raw_source_text: Mutauag: When you are attacked, you may reduce the value of a die in the attack by 1.

cards.name: Alnabi Mosa

abilities.name: Nasiha
abilities.text: Each roll, change each die that is a 1 to a 3, and each die that is a 6 to a 4.

cards.flavor: Chosen by Maowiva to preach to her people, Moses was conveyed by order of Emperor Valens from his hermit life to the city of Alexandria, where he received Episcopal grace.
cards.raw_source_text: Nasiha: Each roll, change each die that is a 1 to a 3, and each die that is a 6 to a 4.

cards.name: Sahm

abilities.name: Taan
abilities.text: When you attack with this weapon, you may choose to roll one less die, If you do, add your initiative to the attack roll.

cards.flavor: Valuing mobility over weighty physical defenses, the Bedouins employed the javelin extensively in all aspects of their warfare.
cards.raw_source_text: Taan: When you attack with this weapon, you may choose to roll one less die, If you do, add your initiative to the attack roll.

cards.name: Gabal el Taur

abilities.name: Lammah
abilities.text: Once each turn, you may re-roll one die of one of your defense rolls. If you have another cavalry card in play, once each round, you may re-roll an opponent's defense roll. If you have two cards of the same type in play, discard this card.

cards.flavor: To be as swift as the wind is to be as equally uncatchable.
cards.raw_source_text: Lammah: Once each turn, you may re-roll one die of one of your defense rolls. If you have another cavalry card in play, once each round, you may re-roll an opponent's defense roll. If you have two cards of the same type in play, discard this card.

cards.name: Mesbah

abilities.name: Tassannut
abilities.text: When you attack, the total defense roll bonus cannot be greater than +1.

abilities.name: Sharr
abilities.text: When you roll doubles on any roll, your next roll gains +2.

cards.flavor: The “ships of the desert” were also put to more bellicose use. Against enemy cavalry, their smell and size often panicked enemy horses.
cards.raw_source_text:
Tassannut: When you attack, the total defense roll bonus cannot be greater than +1. 
Sharr: When you roll doubles on any roll, your next roll gains +2.

cards.name: Salah ad-Din

abilities.name: Saria
abilities.text: Once each round, move two spaces.

cards.flavor: Saladin was an honorable yet implacable opponent, who elicited the highest respect from friend and foe alike. He recaptured Jerusalem after 88 years of Crusader rule.
cards.background: Born to a Kurdish family near the river Tigris, Salah ad-Din began his military career in service to Nur ad-Din against the Fatimid caliphate of Egypt. After becoming vizier of the caliphate, he succeeded in the extraordinarily difficult task of protecting Egypt against the advances of Amalric I. With the death of the caliph, Salah ad-Din assumed power, revitalising Egypt both militarily and economically. Upon the death of Nur ad-Din, he declared himself Sultan of Egypt. Turning his attention towards the Crusader kingdom, he secured rapid victories, capturing Jerusalem and swiftly taking all Crusader-held cities with the exception of Tyre. A man of incredible tolerance, and renowned for his great generosity and humanism, he was universally lauded in both East and West as the epitome of a noble warrior. Use Salah ad-Din, the paragon of honor, to defeat your foes with the virtue born of a true heart, so that none may question your rule over the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Saria - Action: Once each round, move two spaces.

cards.name: Al Madina al Mokadassa

abilities.name: Bait El Arwah
abilities.text: All player's attacks deal +1 damage. All player's defense rolls gain +2 and may gain no other bonuses.

cards.flavor: Over three millennia old, Jerusalem is sacred to the world’s three major monotheistic religions, and has been fought over almost since its founding.
cards.raw_source_text: Bait El Arwah: All player's attacks deal +1 damage. All player's defense rolls gain +2 and may gain no other bonuses.

cards.name: Saif Damashki

abilities.name: Hadid Malfuf
abilities.text: When you attack with this weapon, the defender rolls one extra die. You may choose one die to exclude from the defense roll.

cards.flavor: The beautiful, rippling patterns that typify the Damascene forging technique belie the deadly effectiveness of these folded steel blades.
cards.raw_source_text: Hadid Malfuf: When you attack with this weapon, the defender rolls one extra die. You may choose one die to exclude from the defense roll.

cards.name: Bargustuwan

abilities.name: Derra Elhosan
abilities.text: Your cavalry cards cannot be discarded by an opponent’s card abilities.

abilities.name: Amr Belmotarada
abilities.text: Once each round, after an opponent uses an action ability, or moves out of a space in your warrior’s basic attack grid, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: A simple, quilted covering, the bargustuwan provided protection for a mount while remaining sufficiently light as to leave the horse’s maneuverability unhindered.
cards.raw_source_text:
Derra Elhosan: Your cavalry cards cannot be discarded by an opponent’s card abilities. 
Amr Belmotarada: Once each round, after an opponent uses an action ability, or moves out of a space in your warrior’s basic attack grid, you may move one space.

cards.name: Khaimat Hemaya Men Alraml

abilities.name: Ghadab
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +2. When you attack, if the defense roll is doubles, discard this card after the attack.

cards.flavor: Adorned with his personal emblem, Saladin’s shield was as much a metaphor for the defense of his kingdom and Islam as it was a means of personal protection.
cards.raw_source_text: Ghadab: Your attack rolls gain +2. When you attack, if the defense roll is doubles, discard this card after the attack.

cards.name: Tariq ibn Ziyad

abilities.name: Takawa
abilities.text: All of your rolls get –1. Reduce all damage you take by 1, to a minimum of 1.

cards.flavor: Leading the conquest of Visigothic Hispania in 711, Tariq, a Berber Muslim and Umayyad general, came to be known as Taric el Tuerto (Tariq the one–eyed).
cards.background: N/A Promo Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Takawa: All of your rolls get –1. Reduce all damage you take by 1, to a minimum of 1.

cards.name: Hadis Kaiim

abilities.name: Khauf Aledrab
abilities.text: Before initiative is determined each round, roll two dice. If this value is greater than any opponent’s experience, that opponent gets –2 initiative each round while this card is in play. Otherwise, you gain +3 experience.

cards.flavor: “Where would you flee? The sea waits behind you, and the enemy before you: so, by God, all that is left to you is forthright determination, and you have no minister now but your swords,” – Tariq ibn Ziyad, recounted by al–Maqqari.
cards.raw_source_text: Khauf Aledrab: Before initiative is determined each round, roll two dice. If this value is greater than any opponent’s experience, that opponent gets –2 initiative each round while this card is in play. Otherwise, you gain +3 experience.

cards.name: Balltat Harb

abilities.name: La Mubalie
abilities.text: You may attack twice each round with this weapon if the defender has no support cards that require hands to use.

abilities.name: Kataa
abilities.text: The second attack made with this weapon each round deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Almost five feet (150 cm) long, this heavy, two-handed war axe was a lethal weapon in the hands of a trained warrior.
cards.raw_source_text:
La Mubalie: You may attack twice each round with this weapon if the defender has no support cards that require hands to use. 
Kataa: The second attack made with this weapon each round deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Khauza

abilities.name: Harab
abilities.text: When you would take damage from an opponent’s card ability, if you have that amount or more experience, you may lose that much experience. If you do, reduce the damage to 0.

cards.flavor: Muslim armor continued to be primarily constructed from mail right up to the nineteenth century. The helmet was one of the few pieces, along with greaves and vambraces, where plate was preferred.
cards.raw_source_text: Harab: When you would take damage from an opponent’s card ability, if you have that amount or more experience, you may lose that much experience. If you do, reduce the damage to 0.

cards.name: Jabal Tariq

abilities.name: Mohattem Elamwag – Reveal
abilities.text: Place this card in your warrior’s space.

abilities.name: Maazul
abilities.text: Attack rolls made against a warrior in this space cannot gain a larger bonus than the defender’s bonus to the defense roll.

cards.flavor: Tariq Ibn Ziyad began his conquest of Spain from this small mountain. Today, Jabal Tariq, the “Rock of Tariq,” is better known as Gibraltar.
cards.raw_source_text:
Mohattem Elamwag – Reveal: Place this card in your warrior’s space. 
Maazul: Attack rolls made against a warrior in this space cannot gain a larger bonus than the defender’s bonus to the defense roll.

cards.name: Carlos V

abilities.name: Posición Legítima
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you lose initiative, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: Holy Roman Emperor and the first King of Spain, Charles V waged extensive wars against France and Italy, as well as the Ottoman Empire.
cards.background: Charles V reigned as Holy Roman Emperor, the first King of Spain (as Charles I) and as the Archduke of Austria. His rule was beset with problems, with the Reformation causing enormous religious unrest and the armies of France and Turkey constantly threatening the borders of his demesne. His campaigns in Italy inadvertently led to the sacking of Rome, which incurred the wrath of the Pope, but also gave him the leverage he needed to prevent the annulment of his aunt’s marriage to King Henry VIII of England. Charles outlawed Protestantism, but was unable to follow up the edict with action, due to continuing aggression from a Turkish-French alliance. After 40 years of rule, during which he overcame obstacles that would have felled lesser men, he abdicated and retired to a monastery. Use Charles V, Emperor, King and Archduke, to calmly thwart the attacks of any enemy, to hold fast, and to guard your rightful rulership over the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Posición Legítima: At the start of each round, if you lose initiative, gain 1 life.

cards.name: Mercurino Gattinara

abilities.name: Incitar Asalto – Reveal
abilities.text: Your defense rolls this round get –1. Your attack rolls this round gain +3.

cards.flavor: An Italian statesman and jurist, Gattinara served as a legal advisor, a counselor to Margaret of Austria, a chancellor to Charles V and a Roman Catholic Cardinal.
cards.raw_source_text: Incitar Asalto – Reveal: Your defense rolls this round get –1. Your attack rolls this round gain +3.

cards.name: Albarda Espanola

abilities.name: Perímetro Impenetrable
abilities.text: Once each round, when an opponent moves into a space in this weapon’s attack grid, you may make an attack with this weapon.

cards.flavor: The halberd consisted of an axe blade mounted on a long shaft and topped with a spike. A hook mounted behind the axe blade was used to topple mounted opponents.
cards.raw_source_text: Perímetro Impenetrable: Once each round, when an opponent moves into a space in this weapon’s attack grid, you may make an attack with this weapon.

cards.name: Escudo de Carlos V

abilities.name: Mantener Terreno
abilities.text: When you are attacked, the attacker cannot re–roll any die. Reduce the damage of all critical hits made against you to 1.

cards.flavor: Since 1438, and with the exception of Charles VII in 1742, the electors of the Holy Roman Empire always chose an emperor from the Hapsburg dynasty, to the extent that the title became akin to a family fiefdom.
cards.raw_source_text: Mantener Terreno: When you are attacked, the attacker cannot re–roll any die. Reduce the damage of all critical hits made against you to 1.

cards.name: Ballestero Vasco

abilities.name: Desplegar – Reveal
abilities.text: Place this card in any space in the arena. If an opposing warrior ever moves into this card’s space, discard this card.

abilities.name: Disparar – Action
abilities.text: Once each round, make a basic attack at +2 against any opposing warrior not adjacent to this card’s space.

cards.flavor: The deployment of Basque crossbowmen proved decisive in many of Charles V’s battles against the French and Italians.
cards.raw_source_text:
Desplegar – Reveal: Place this card in any space in the arena. If an opposing warrior ever moves into this card’s space, discard this card. 
Disparar – Action: Once each round, make a basic attack at +2 against any opposing warrior not adjacent to this card’s space.

cards.name: El Cid

abilities.name: Maniobra Estratégica
abilities.text: Once each round, before you make an attack or use an action ability, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: One of the leading generals in the Reconquista, El Cid became a mercenary for both sides after the jealous King Alfonso VI exiled him. El Cid later captured the city of Valencia, ruling it until his death in 1099.
cards.background: Born to a family of minor nobility, Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar entered military service to retake Spain in the Reconquista. His bravery and resourcefulness quickly earned him the highest respect among both Christian and Islamic forces. El Cid was a humble commander, and was known to gather suggestions from his troops so as to best avoid making a mistake himself. However, El Cid’s disrespect towards authority and his continuing successes angered King Alfonso VI, who cast him into exile. El Cid then served as a mercenary for both sides until, having gathered an army of both Christian and Muslim soldiers, he conquered the city of Valencia. He ruled the city until his death in 1099. Use El Cid Campeador, respected by friend and foe alike, to carve out your own kingdom on the sands of the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Maniobra Estratégica: Once each round, before you make an attack or use an action ability, you may move one space.

cards.name: Santiago Matamoros

abilities.name: Confesión
abilities.text: Once each round, when you are attacked and the attacker rolls doubles, the attack deals –1 damage, to a minimum of 1, and you gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: St. James is said to have preached in Spain before returning to Judea, where he was executed. Tradition holds that, during the Reconquista, he miraculously appeared to lead the Christian army to victory at the Battle of Clavijo.
cards.raw_source_text: Confesión: Once each round, when you are attacked and the attacker rolls doubles, the attack deals –1 damage, to a minimum of 1, and you gain 1 life.

cards.name: Tizona y Colada

abilities.name: Ataque en Pareja – Action
abilities.text: Once each round, if you have attacked with this weapon and moved this turn, make an attack with this weapon against an adjacent opponent at +2.

cards.flavor: A metallurgical analysis of El Cid’s sword “Tizona” showed that the blade was probably made in Moorish Cordoba. It can be seen today in the Museo del Ejército, Madrid.
cards.raw_source_text: Ataque en Pareja – Action: Once each round, if you have attacked with this weapon and moved this turn, make an attack with this weapon against an adjacent opponent at +2.

cards.name: Cota Corta

abilities.name: Afortunado
abilities.text: When you are attacked with a weapon, if you have less experience than the attacker, your defense roll gains +1.

abilities.name: Malla Resistente
abilities.text: While you are in an opponent’s basic attack grid and he or she is not in your basic attack grid, your defense rolls gain +2.

cards.flavor: “El Cid Campeador” is derived from the Andalusi–Arabic term “al–sidi,” meaning “sir” or “lord” and the Spanish “el campeador”, meaning “army champion”.
cards.raw_source_text:
Afortunado: When you are attacked with a weapon, if you have less experience than the attacker, your defense roll gains +1. 
Malla Resistente: While you are in an opponent’s basic attack grid and he or she is not in your basic attack grid, your defense rolls gain +2.

cards.name: Babieca

abilities.name: Galope
abilities.text: Once each round, if you are a Wind warrior, after you are hit with an attack, you may move to any unoccupied space adjacent to the attacker.

cards.flavor: Pedro El Grande, El Cid’s godfather, gifted El Cid with his choice of horse. Upon choosing what seemed a weak and unsuitable stallion, Pedro exclaimed “Babieca!” meaning “Stupid!”
cards.raw_source_text: Galope: Once each round, if you are a Wind warrior, after you are hit with an attack, you may move to any unoccupied space adjacent to the attacker.

cards.name: El Gran Capitan

abilities.name: Maestro de Armas
abilities.text: Your attack and defense rolls gain +2 against an opponent with a weapon of the same type as your weapon.

cards.flavor: A general, statesman, and flamboyant warrior, Gonzalo Fernández de Córdoba is known as “the father of trench warfare” and is regarded as one of the first modern generals.
cards.background: Gonzalo Fernández de Córdoba, known as El Gran Capitán distinguished himself in the service of the Grand Master of Santiago. His near-reckless bravery in battle was carefully calculated to secure the loyalty of his men by example of courageousness, and a willingness to share every risk endured by his soldiers. After the Granadine campaign, Isabella of Castile sent him to fight against the French in Calabria, where his tactical assessments led to Spanish victory. Moving north, he fought the Turks, and then later the French again, by whom he was greatly outnumbered. His deployment of trench fortifications, shrewdness, and exceptional patience again secured victory, as his enemies eventually overextended themselves. Use Gonzalo Fernández de Córdoba, El Gran Capitán, to tempt your enemies into foolish action. Then, when they rush to take the Anachronism arena, make them realize that it was never theirs to begin with.
cards.raw_source_text: Maestro de Armas: Your attack and defense rolls gain +2 against an opponent with a weapon of the same type as your weapon.

cards.name: Vasallo de la Reina

abilities.name: Decreto Imperial – Reveal
abilities.text: Roll one die. If it is equal or greater than your life, move an opposing warrior one space. If this moves them into a space in any of your attack grids, you may immediately make an attack against them.

cards.flavor: After the death of Don Alfonso, the king’s brother, Córdoba dedicated his service to Isabella of Castile. He commanded her escort and, at Granada, defeated an enemy sortie before her eyes.
cards.raw_source_text: Decreto Imperial – Reveal: Roll one die. If it is equal or greater than your life, move an opposing warrior one space. If this moves them into a space in any of your attack grids, you may immediately make an attack against them.

cards.name: Pica de Infante

abilities.name: Desgarrar
abilities.text: Once each round, after an opponent uses an action to move into a space in this weapon’s attack grid, roll two dice. If the total is less than the opponent’s experience, they must discard one of their non–weapon support cards.

cards.flavor: Fighting against the French in Calabria, Córdoba rearmed many of his troops with pikes after it became apparent that a heavier punch was needed on the battlefield.
cards.raw_source_text: Desgarrar: Once each round, after an opponent uses an action to move into a space in this weapon’s attack grid, roll two dice. If the total is less than the opponent’s experience, they must discard one of their non–weapon support cards.

cards.name: Armadura Completa

abilities.name: Irrompible – Reveal
abilities.text: For this round, weapon attacks made against you deal –2 damage, to a minimum of 0.

cards.flavor: The Grand Master of Santiago, Alonso de Cárdenas, praised the splendor of Córdoba’s armor, saying he was conspicuous on the field due both to his bravery and the armor’s magnificence.
cards.raw_source_text: Irrompible – Reveal: For this round, weapon attacks made against you deal –2 damage, to a minimum of 0.

cards.name: Formacion de Piqueros

abilities.name: Piquero
abilities.text: Your attack rolls with polearms gain +2.

abilities.name: Trabajo en Equipo – Reveal
abilities.text: You may choose another warrior. That warrior’s attacks with polearms deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: The veterans of Córdoba’s Granadine campaign were used to fighting in loose formation. Retraining with pikes entailed a change in formation and tactics.
cards.raw_source_text:
Piquero: Your attack rolls with polearms gain +2.
Trabajo en Equipo – Reveal: You may choose another warrior. That warrior’s attacks with polearms deal +1 damage.

cards.name: Hernan Cortes

abilities.name: Reflejos Rápidos
abilities.text: Your attack and defense rolls gain +1 against warriors with more speed than your warrior.

cards.flavor: Initially taking part in the conquests of Hispaniola and Cuba, Cortés famously went on to conquer Mexico and the Aztec Empire in the name of Spain.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Reflejos Rápidos: Your attack and defense rolls gain +1 against warriors with more speed than your warrior.

cards.name: La Malinche

abilities.name: Negociar
abilities.text: Once each round, when you make a roll, if no opponent has a card that matches your warrior’s culture, you may re–roll one die of any player’s attack or defense roll. If you are an Earth or Metal warrior, you may give the re–roll +1 or –1.

cards.flavor: A native Mesoamerican woman, La Malinche accompanied Cortes on his conquest of Mexico and bore him a son.  The term “La Malinche” lives on today as a byword for treachery.
cards.raw_source_text: Negociar: Once each round, when you make a roll, if no opponent has a card that matches your warrior’s culture, you may re–roll one die of any player’s attack or defense roll. If you are an Earth or Metal warrior, you may give the re–roll +1 or –1.

cards.name: Arcabuz

abilities.name: Abrir Fuego
abilities.text: Attacking with this weapon requires an additional action.

abilities.name: Diana
abilities.text: When you attack with this weapon, if the attack roll is even and the attack hits, it deals +2 damage.

cards.flavor: A smoothbore, matchlock firearm, the harquebus was usually fired with the barrel hooked on a raised, fork–like stand.
cards.raw_source_text:
Abrir Fuego: Attacking with this weapon requires an additional action. 
Diana: When you attack with this weapon, if the attack roll is even and the attack hits, it deals +2 damage.

cards.name: Hombreras

abilities.name: Desviar
abilities.text: When you make an attack roll, if any die is a 1, your defense rolls on the defender’s next turn gain +1.

cards.flavor: Pauldrons were a development of earlier plate spaulders. Pauldrons tended to extend below the line of the armpit, and could cover areas of the back and chest as well.
cards.raw_source_text: Desviar: When you make an attack roll, if any die is a 1, your defense rolls on the defender’s next turn gain +1.

cards.name: Oro Azteca

abilities.name: Tentar
abilities.text: At the start of each round, roll one die. If the result is greater than the number of your face–down support cards, your attacks deal +1 damage that round.

cards.flavor:
We wander here and there in our desolate poverty. 
We are mortal men. We have seen bloodshed and pain 
Where once we saw beauty and valor – Cantares Mexicanos
cards.raw_source_text: Tentar: At the start of each round, roll one die. If the result is greater than the number of your face–down support cards, your attacks deal +1 damage that round.

cards.name: Isabella I of Castile

abilities.name: Sanción
abilities.text: Players cannot use abilities on weapon cards. At the start of each round, if you did not win initiative and have less life than all opponents, this ability affects only your opponents that round.

cards.flavor: Ruling in Spain with her husband, Ferdinand II of Aragon, Isabella re–conquered Granada, funded Columbus’ expedition and established the Spanish Inquisition.
cards.background: After ascending the throne in adverse political circumstances, Isabella and her husband, Ferdinand II of Aragon, became co-rulers of the Iberian Peninsula. In 1478, they established The Spanish Inquisition. Leading the Reconquista in Granada, Isabella and Ferdinand entered the city in 1492 to receive its loyalty. Isabella granted Columbus the funds he required to make his voyage, initiating Spain’s Golden Age of exploration and expansion. The achievements of her reign were remarkable: Spain was united, the church was reformed, the Reconquista was concluded, the legal system was rationalized, and Columbus’ voyage set the stage for Spain to become the first modern superpower. Use Isabella I of Castile to strike at discordance, to look to the future, and to bring all warriors who step into the Anachronism arena under your rule.
cards.raw_source_text: Sanción: Players cannot use abilities on weapon cards. At the start of each round, if you did not win initiative and have less life than all opponents, this ability affects only your opponents that round.

cards.name: Fernando II de Aragon

abilities.name: Pasión
abilities.text: If you have a female card in play, your attack rolls gain +2 and critical hits against you deal +1 damage.

abilities.name: Piedad
abilities.text: You may play another Inspiration card in this game.

cards.flavor: The co–rule of Ferdinand II of Aragon and Isabella I of Castile made their kingdoms the most powerful in Europe.
cards.raw_source_text:
Pasión: If you have a female card in play, your attack rolls gain +2 and critical hits against you deal +1 damage. 
Piedad: You may play another Inspiration card in this game.

cards.name: Ropera Toledana

abilities.name: Impulso – Action
abilities.text: Make an attack with this weapon, if possible.

cards.flavor: From the falcata blades of the fifth century BC to the rapiers of the sixteenth century AD, Toledan steel was renowned across the world for its incredible quality.
cards.raw_source_text: Impulso – Action: Make an attack with this weapon, if possible.

cards.name: Fervor Religioso

abilities.name: Impulso Vital
abilities.text: Opponents may not use abilities on items or mystic cards.

cards.flavor: Devout in the extreme, Isabella and Ferdinand embarked on the spiritual unification of Spain, garnering the title of “Catholic Monarchs” from Pope Alexander VI.
cards.raw_source_text: Impulso Vital: Opponents may not use abilities on items or mystic cards.

cards.name: La Inquisicion Espanola

abilities.name: Confiscar – Reveal
abilities.text: You may discard a tome.

abilities.name: Perseguir
abilities.text: Once each turn, after an opposing warrior uses an ability on a Deity card, you may make a basic attack on that warrior at +2.

cards.flavor: Although the Treaty of Granada assured religious tolerance for Muslims, it was not long before they, along with the Jews, were persecuted for their beliefs and forced to convert or flee Spain.
cards.raw_source_text:
Confiscar – Reveal: You may discard a tome. 
Perseguir: Once each turn, after an opposing warrior uses an ability on a Deity card, you may make a basic attack on that warrior at +2.
